strengths: Strong SQL and Python ETL experience
Proven operational impact with quantified metrics
Experience integrating APIs and data sources
Dashboards and data visualization expertise (Power BI/Tableau)
weaknesses: Relationship-building examples are implicit, not detailed
Limited years in long-tenured customer-facing roles (2–6 window borderline)
improvement_bullets: Collaborated cross-functionally with Underwriting and Product teams to deploy AI-enabled solutions, ensuring platform features directly addressed enterprise-scale business needs.
Managed client-facing relationships as an Operations Lead, translating data workflows into actionable SaaS-based improvements that increased customer acquisition by 300%.
Partnered with stakeholders to integrate enterprise software APIs, streamlining data pipeline reliability and enhancing the overall value of our data-driven products.
Led technical implementation and operational strategies, leveraging AI-powered tools to optimize data modeling and bridge the gap between complex engineering and end-user business requirements.
Cultivated strong relationships with clients to understand their operational challenges, providing strategic guidance on utilizing data analytics tools to drive performance.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Deployment Strategist opportunity with Stott and May. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's focus on building an AI-powered operating system for the federal landscape, which sits at the perfect intersection of my technical background and passion for solving complex, large-scale operational challenges. What interests me about working for Stott and May is its approach to consolidating fragmented market and program data into a single, cohesive AI-enabled platform. This mission excites me because I have spent my career transforming raw data into high-impact, actionable business intelligence. I believe my expertise in data-driven operational improvement and my experience with enterprise software integrations would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex technical requirements into clear, value-driven solutions. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our client onboarding process; I developed and implemented an automated workflow using API integrations and data modeling that reduced processing time by 40% and saved 8 hours of labor per week. This initiative directly enabled our team to reallocate resources toward scaling customer acquisition, resulting in a 300% growth over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Deployment Strategist role at Stott and May would allow me to bring my data workflow expertise to the implementation of your AI-powered solutions. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my relationship-building and cross-functional collaboration skills. By partnering with Underwriting and Product teams to align software features with business objectives, I helped reduce project delivery delays by 25%. This experience honed my ability to navigate the ambiguity often found in enterprise environments. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in technical project management and data analysis in working on the deployment of your platform at Stott and May. I know that my proficiency in Python/SQL and my focus on client success align with what it takes to succeed in the Deployment Strategist role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
